name: clodex
description: Autonomous development loop: plan, ship, run codex adversarial-review, and iterate on findings until approved or threshold/iteration cap is reached. v0.3.1 REVERTS the v0.3.0 PowerShell-preferred pivot — Bash is the canonical invocation shell again, matching the proven-working v0.2.5–v0.2.9 pattern validated end-to-end in the May 14 PR #77 production run. v0.3.0's 'mechanism flexibility' (PowerShell preferred on Windows) and '3-retry exponential-backoff stall recovery' both proved counterproductive when investigation showed the same broker process today serving successful Bash-invoked manual calls and failing PowerShell-invoked clodex calls. v0.3.1 restores Bash-mandatory invocation, restores single-retry broker-wedge recovery (kill broker + retry once before escalating to codex-reproducible-hang), and preserves Phase 0.6 orphaned-state cleanup (now Bash-invoked; surfaces MSYS bug per Rule 4 instead of silently re-routing). PowerShell `Stop-Process` is retained ONLY as the narrow sanctioned carve-out for broker-kill recovery (because Git Bash's `taskkill /PID` fallback hits the MSYS path-mangling bug). The `< /dev/null` stdin closure on the launch is preserved from v0.3.0 as the one durable defense-in-depth bit. Paired with HARD_RULES.md v0.3.1. v0.2.9 lifted --version/--help into commands/clodex.md. v0.2.8 added broker wedge auto-recovery. v0.2.7 added iter-NNN-stalled.md breadcrumbs + ~3-min stall detection. v0.2.6 added per-iteration iter-NNN-review.md. v0.2.5 externalized hard rules. v0.2.1 adapted to codex plugin v1.0.4's disable-model-invocation. v0.2 baseline: hard rules, --continue flag.
